title: The Sunshine Of Thine Eyes
content:
The sunshine of thine eyes,
    (O still, celestial beam!)
    Whatever it touches it fills
    With the life of its lambent gleam.

    The sunshine of thine eyes,
    O let it fall on me!
    Though I be but a mote of the air,
    I could turn to gold for thee!
meaning:
George Parsons Lathrop's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Sunshine Of Thine Eyes"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
